{{Infobox location
| name = Shadow Ark
| introduced = The Engineer
}}

''See also: [[Shadow Ark Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Shadow]] [[Ark]] is a [[Zenith Device]] and a [[Zenith Colony]] that functions as a massive, highly secure repository and sanctuary. Characterized by its obsidian and violet appearance, the entity can manifest as a faceted jewel or expand into a vast environment featuring a fragmented purple sky. This internal space is large enough to house entire star systems, providing a safe haven for refugees and inhabitants from various worlds. Its advanced [[Zenith Era technology]] allows it to maintain an atmosphere and provides a level of concealment so absolute that it can remain hidden from magic, [[Aethertech]], scanners, and even history itself. Beyond its role as a shelter, it serves as a strategic staging ground and contains valuable materials such as synapse nodes and thought-conduction alloy. [[Shadow]] [[Ark]] possesses a sentient personality and can take the form of a muscular human wearing violet-and-black armor with a crown of horns. While it is designed to protect its inhabitants from threats like the [[D’Niss]] or [[the Perfected]], many of its functions are restricted until it establishes a mortal bond with a [[Gatekeeper]]. This designated Guardian is expected to be a champion of the colony's people, serving as an ultimate protector and mediator for those living within the [[Ark]]. Although it can be captured or contained, its structural integrity is such that any attempt to breach its defenses would likely result in its destruction.}}

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[The Shadow]] [[Ark]] is a piece of [[Aethertech]] and a [[Zenith Device]] that identifies as the [[Zenith Colony]]. While it serves as a target for the crew to locate with [[Murrok]]'s assistance, this non-weaponized device contains all the thought-conduction alloy and synapse nodes in the galaxy. Upon activation by a guardian, this [[diamond]]-shaped obsidian structure can expand into a massive entity that guides a superior fleet and flies alongside [[Starhammer]] in [[Subspace]]. The vessel possesses a disembodied voice and can unfold into fractal shards of black and purple, capable of consuming space or restricting [[Aether]]. Ultimately, the [[Shadow]] [[Ark]] acts as a reliable location where magic can be used, though it is noted to be [[Horizon]]'s brother who does not like to share.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] is a [[Zenith Device]] that can hide.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c25">''The Engineer'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] activates when someone is found to act as a guardian for its territory.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[The Shadow]] [[Ark]] is a target that the crew hopes to find with [[Murrok]]'s help.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c29">''The Engineer'', chapter 29</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[The Shadow]] [[Ark]] contains all the synapse nodes and thought-conduction alloy in the galaxy.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c29" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[The Shadow]] [[Ark]] is not a weapon.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c30">''The Engineer'', chapter 30</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[The Shadow]] [[Ark]] could potentially take orders from [[Starhammer]] based on personal judgment.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c30" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] is a [[Zenith Device]] and identifies as the [[Zenith Colony]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c31">''The Engineer'', chapter 31</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] is a piece of [[Aethertech]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c31" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] is a potential course of action if repairs cannot be found.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c32">''The Engineer'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[The Shadow]] [[Ark]] guides a superior fleet.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c33">''The Engineer'', chapter 33</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] can expand into a massive structure.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c33" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[The Shadow]] [[Ark]] is shaped like a cut [[diamond]] with corners that unfold into a tunnel of violet light.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c34">''The Engineer'', chapter 34</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] is a ship commanded by [[Starhammer]] that possesses a disembodied voice.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c35">''The Engineer'', chapter 35</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] is an outlier in the battle.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c36">''The Engineer'', chapter 36</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] is a starship that flies alongside [[Starhammer]] in [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c36" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] can cause a disturbance that includes bright violet light.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c36" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[The Shadow]] [[Ark]] has glistening obsidian jaws and can implode into [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c38">''The Engineer'', chapter 38</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] is an entity or location that swallowed [[Starhammer]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c39">''The Engineer'', chapter 39</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] is a [[diamond]]-shaped obsidian structure that burns with purple fury.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c45">''The Engineer'', chapter 45</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] spoke to [[Starhammer]] in the swirls of [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c45" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] can unfold into a thousand fractal shards of black and purple.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47">''The Engineer'', chapter 47</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] can consume space/warp.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] can project his voice.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] can restrict [[Aether]] in a space.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]]'s sky flickered with violet lightning.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] points out that [[Horizon]] will go dormant if the [[Captain]] dies.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c48">''The Engineer'', chapter 48</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] notes that people know where [[Mell]] is.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c48" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] is a place where magic can be used.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c51">''The Engineer'', chapter 51</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] called for [[Shadow Ark]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c54">''The Engineer'', chapter 54</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] is [[Horizon]]'s brother.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c55">''The Engineer'', chapter 55</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] is more reliable than [[Horizon]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c55" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Shadow Ark]] and [[Horizon]] do not like to share.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c55" />}}

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Shadow]] [[Ark]] exists as an obsidian crystal capable of unfolding into a short man adorned in violet-edged obsidian armor and a crown of horns. While his attention remains focused on his inner world, many of his functions stay restricted until he establishes a mortal bond. He possesses non-adaptive systems and is capable of making a buzzing sound similar to clearing his throat. Amidst a fractured black-and-violet sky, he eventually begins accepting evacuating ships.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Shadow Ark]]'s attention is focused on his inner world.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c17">''The Knight'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Many of [[Shadow Ark]]'s functions are restricted until he has a mortal bond.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Shadow Ark]] can expand into a short man with violet-edged obsidian armor and a crown of horns.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Shadow Ark]] possesses systems that are not adaptive like the [[Zenith Cannon]]'s.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c22">''The Knight'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Shadow Ark]] is a character capable of buzzing as though clearing his throat.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c23">''The Knight'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Shadow Ark]] is accepting evacuating ships.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c24">''The Knight'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Shadow Ark]] is an obsidian crystal that can unfold into a man in violet-edged obsidian armor.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c38">''The Knight'', chapter 38</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Shadow Ark]] has a fractured black-and-violet sky.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c41">''The Knight'', chapter 41</ref>}}

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Shadow]] [[Ark]] is a [[Zenith Colony]] and a Guardian of Shyrax that serves as a secure cargo transport ship capable of swallowing planets to hide. This starship, which houses the [[Titan Force headquarters]] and a sealed sky, can take a human form featuring crown-like spikes on its scalp. After [[Horizon]] sent [[Omega]] to the vessel, it was eventually stolen by enemies, though it remains a location where people desire to settle and where [[Nova-Bots]] reside. The black-and-violet entity can unfold into a spatial warp or expand into a massive wall, and it has even swallowed the moon that supported the [[Titan Force]].}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Shadow Ark]] is the name of the [[Zenith Colony]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c6">''The Pilot'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Shadow Ark]] is a location where Shyrax's children could be kept safe.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Shadow Ark]] is a Guardian of Shyrax.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c14">''The Pilot'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Shadow Ark]] can swallow planets and hide.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Shadow Ark]] is a starship that contains the [[Titan Force headquarters]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c15">''The Pilot'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Horizon]] sent [[Omega]] to [[Shadow Ark]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Shadow Ark]] has a human form with spikes on its scalp like a crown.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Shadow Ark]] is a vessel that [[Solstice]] has not yet attempted to open.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c16">''The Pilot'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Omega]] is inside [[Shadow Ark]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Shadow]] [[Ark]] is a starship that can groan with exasperation.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c18">''The Pilot'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Shadow Ark]] has a sealed sky.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Shadow Ark]] may have been sent to the [[Karoshan]] facility by [[Solstice]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c20">''The Pilot'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Shadow Ark]] is a ship that can fall into enemy hands.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Shadow Ark]] was stolen by enemies.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c21">''The Pilot'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Shadow Ark]] is a secure cargo transport ship.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c24">''The Pilot'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The hostages may be sheltered in [[Shadow Ark]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Shadow Ark]] expanded into a massive wall to cover one flank of the narrator's ships.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c28">''The Pilot'', chapter 28</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The crew had to pick up the [[Shadow Ark]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c30">''The Pilot'', chapter 30</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Shadow Ark]] swallowed the moon that supported the [[Titan Force]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c32">''The Pilot'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Shadow Ark]] is a place where people want to settle.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c36">''The Pilot'', chapter 36</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Shadow Ark]] is a location where [[Nova-Bots]] stay.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c46">''The Pilot'', chapter 46</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Shadow Ark]] is a black-and-violet shape that unfolds in the air and into a spatial warp.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c49">''The Pilot'', chapter 49</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|"This is it," Shadow Ark said to him, the sound weirdly distorted in the swirls of Subspace.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c45" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|“If the Captain dies, Horizon will go dormant,” Shadow Ark pointed out.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c48" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|“Just because you are my Guardian doesn’t mean you can haul me around whenever you like!” Shadow Ark demanded.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c14" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|“We haven’t even attempted to open Shadow Ark,” said the Voice of Solstice.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c16" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|“This facility could be where they’ve sent Shadow Ark,” I said.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c20" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{The Last Horizon nav}}

[[Category:Locations]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon locations]]
[[Category:Structures]]
